heroText: Minimalism in UI design is the intentional reduction of elements to their most essential form. It prioritizes negative space and high-contrast typography to eliminate cognitive noise.

history.title: The Bauhaus
history.description: Founded in Germany, the Bauhaus school pioneered the 'form follows function' mantra, stripping away Victorian ornament in favor of geometric essentialism.

history.title: NYC Minimalism
history.description: Artists like Donald Judd and Dan Flavin shifted the focus to industrial materials and literal space, influencing the 'White Space' movement in modern UI.

history.title: Digital Utility
history.description: The launch of the iPhone and the rise of flat design marked the return to Rams-inspired minimalism, prioritizing clarity over skeuomorphic decoration.
